tweets.title: This seems like a bad configuration on @Google's part. They are clearly receiving emails with a spoofed "from" address (of the target victim) and auto-replying to it without checking SPF / DKIM / etc records.
tweets.summary: This seems like a bad configuration on @Google 's part. They are clearly receiving emails with a spoofed "from" address (of the target victim) and auto-replying to it without checking SPF / DKIM / etc records. Jameson Lopp (@lopp) New phishing tactic: attackers are abusing Google

tweets.title: New phishing tactic: attackers are abusing Google's no-reply addresses to send messages to victims that are from Google infrastructure. They can't control the autoresponse email body, so they put their message in the subject line.
